No Poverty.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus links to poverty eradication.

Quality Education.reason: The course is a university-level program (DBA education) providing tertiary education (4.3) and developing research skills for academic careers (4.4). It also fosters knowledge for future academic research (4.7).
evidence: establish a good foundation for future academic research

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course develops research analysis and innovation skills in information management theory, directly supporting higher economic productivity, creativity, and innovation (Targets 8.2, 8.3).
evidence: Ability to analyze research results and the innovation of information management theory

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course explicitly enhances scientific research capabilities and innovation in information management theory, directly aligning with Target 9.5 to encourage innovation and upgrade technological capabilities.
evidence: Ability to analyze research results and the innovation of information management theory

evidence: Social responsibility and business ethics responding to information technology innovation

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course's core competence in 'Social responsibility and business ethics' has an inferred link to reducing corruption (16.5) and building accountable institutions (16.6).
evidence: Social responsibility and business ethics responding to information technology innovation

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course explicitly aims to develop the 'Ability to publish in international academic journals and conferences,' directly supporting global knowledge sharing and international cooperation (Target 17.6).
evidence: Ability to publish in international academic journals and conferences
